feed.title: Piscataquis County Registry of Deeds
feed.subtitle: No dedicated registry news/RSS feed exists; monitored by full-page fingerprint of the official registry page. Standard CMS page with fraud-alert notices and recording fee/requirements PDF link.

entry.title: Page updated: Piscataquis County Registry of Deeds
entry.content:
This page's content changed since the last check. Visit the page directly to review the update.
What changed (text excerpt): Removed: - Home - General Info - Employment Opportunities - Request For Bids - County News - General FAQs - Commissioners - Meeting Agendas - Meeting Minutes - Contact Commissioners Office - Finance - Budget Advisory Committee - Contact Finance - Maintenance - DA - Emergency Mgmt - About LEPC - Preparedness - Training - Forms - E-911 Officer - Contact EMA - Sheriff - In the Community - Investigations / Patrol - Dispatch / Communications - Civil Deputies - County Jail - Contact Sheriff’s Office - Probate - Deeds - Unorganized Territories - General Assistance - Vehicle Registry - Waste - Voting - Liquor License - Tax Valuation - Registry of Deeds - The - Registry of Deeds - receives, records, and maintains an alphabetically indexed library of land records. The hardcopy land record indices begin in 1838 and the indices of plans, plats and surveys begin in the 1800’s. Surveys are referenced by the names of the owners, the surveyor, and the town in which the land is located. The Register of Deeds is an elected position in Piscataquis County government and the term of office is four years. Registry office hours are 8:30am to 4:00pm, Monday — Friday. - The digital indices and images may be viewed at no cost on: - www.maineregistryofdeeds.com - . Click on Maine Registry of Deeds Association (statewide portal) and select Piscataquis County on the map. Please note: Not all document images are avai [diff truncated]

entry.title: Page updated: Piscataquis County Registry of Deeds
entry.content: This page's content changed since the last check. Visit the page directly to review the update.

Surveys are referenced by the names of the owners, the surveyor, and the town in which the land is located. The Register of Deeds is an elected position in Piscataquis County government and the term of office is four years.
